title: Uniface Support for Unicode
text: Uniface Support for Unicode Uniface supports Unicode in the user interface, database storage and retrieval, sorting, case conversion, and data exchange. Uniface uses Unicode UTF-8 encoding for as its internal character set. On GUI platforms, any Unicode characters can appear in the user interface, and be entered into String fields. On any Uniface-supported platform, Unicode characters can be stored in and retrieved from a Unicode-supported database, and exchanged with other Unicode-based components, such as Java and Unicode files. Uniface also supports Unicode sorting, case conversion (sometimes known as bending), and normalization, making it possible to sort and search strings based on the current locale. For more information, see Language and Locale . Uniface uses Unicode UTF-8 encoding for as its internal character set. However, Uniface does not provide the glyphs to display the characters, nor does it supply the tools to enter the characters. These are handled by Microsoft Windows. For data entry and display, Microsoft Windows must be configured to display and input the desired Unicode characters, and Uniface must be configured to use a Unicode Font for displaying Unicode characters. To develop and deploy a Unicode application in Uniface, you need to understand the following: The correct data type and packing code for storing Unicode characters Where Unicode characters are allowed and where not ProcScript functions that can handle Unicode characters Was this topic helpful?
